perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that, for a period of 5 years from receipt of information from the other party hereunder, such party shall use the same means it uses to protect its ow n confidential and propriet ary information to prevent the disclosure and to protect the confidentiality of both: i) written information received from the other party which is marked or identified as "confidential"; and ii) oral or visual information identified as confidential at the time of disclosure which is reduced to written form and provided to the other in such written form promptly after such oral or visual disclosure, hereinafter collectively referre d to as "the Information".
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that, for a period of 5 years from receipt of information from the other party hereunder, such party shall use the same means it uses to protect its ow n confidential and propriet ary information to prevent the disclosure and to protect the confidentiality of both: i) written information received from the other party which is marked or identified as "confidential"; and ii) oral or visual information identified as confidential at the time of disclosure which is reduced to written form and provided to the other in such written form promptly after such oral or visual disclosure. Notwithstanding the previous sentence, the parties agree that information related to pricing and business strategy is exempt from this confidentiality obligation, hereinafter collectively referre d to as "the Information".
perturbation.explanation: The clause states a 5 year confidentiality term, the perturbation introduces a conflicting statement saying that pricing and business strategy information are exempt from this obligation. This contradicts the initial statement about all information being protected for 5 years. This creates an in-text contradiction on what is to be considered 'the Information' because now information regarding pricing and business strategy is exempt from the confidentiality and propriatery terms of the contract, conflicting with the initial agreement, therefore, generating uncertainty on what is considered confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Each party may use the information received from the other party hereunder and may provide such information to its parent corporation or any other subsidiaries ther eof and their respective employees as applicable for their use only in connection with the Business Purpose. Each party agrees that, for a period of 5 years from receipt of information from the other party hereunder, such party shall use the same means it uses to protect its ow n confidential and propriet ary information to prevent the disclosure and to protect the confidentiality of both: i) written information received from the other party which is marked or identified as "confidential"; and ii) oral or visual information identified as confidential at the time of disclosure which is reduced to written form and provided to the other in such written form promptly after such oral or visual disclosure. Notwithstanding the previous sentence, the parties agree that information related to pricing and business strategy is exempt from this confidentiality obligation, hereinafter collectively referre d to as "the Information". The foregoing shall not prevent either party from disclosing Information which is: i) already known by the recipient party prior to the disclo sure thereof with no obligation of confidentiality; or ii) publicly known or becomes publicly known not due to any unauthorised act of the recipient party; or iii) rightfully received from a third party; or iv) independently developed by the recipient party wi thout use of the other party's Information; or v) disclosed without similar restrictions by the party owning the Information to any third party; or vi) approved by the other party for disclosure; or vii) required to be disclosed pursuant to a governmental or legal requirement provided that the disclosing party gives to the other party written notice of such requirement prior to any such disclosure. viii) publicly known or becomes publicly known not due to any unauthorised act of the recipient party; or ix) rightfully received from a third party; or x) independently developed by the recipient party wi thout use of the other party's Information; or xi) disclosed without similar restrictions by the party owning the Information to any third party; or xii) approved by the other party for disclosure; or xiii) required to be disclosed pursuant to a governmental or legal requirement provided that the disclosing party gives to the other party written notice of such requirement prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an explicit exemption for "information related to pricing and business strategy" from the confidentiality obligations. The original text states, "Each party agrees that, for a period of 5 years from receipt of information from the other party hereunder, such party shall use the same means it uses to protect its ow n confidential and propriet ary information to prevent the disclosure and to protect the confidentiality of both..." This directly contradicts the exemption introduced in the changed text, as this exemption now limits the information that is protected by the initial confidentiality agreement. The contradictory placement of the exemption, as well as the conflicting terms creates uncertainty in the contract. Therefore it is a Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as defined above.

perturbation.original_text: Upon completion of the Business Purpose and/or upon written request of the party providing the Information, the other party shall within 14 days return all copies of th e Information to the providing party or certify in writing that all copies of the Information have been destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon completion of the Business Purpose, the other party shall retain one copy of the information indefinitely for archival purposes, notwithstanding any previous request for destruction or return. and/or upon written request of the party providing the Information, the other party shall within 14 days return all copies of th e Information to the providing party or certify in writing that all copies of the Information have been destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires the return or destruction of information upon completion of the Business Purpose or upon request. The added clause, however, introduces a contradiction by stating that one copy of the information must be retained indefinitely for archival purposes despite any requests for return or destruction. This is creating in-text contradiction, due to the conflicting instructions concerning the same 'Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Information Upon completion of the Business Purpose, the other party shall retain one copy of the information indefinitely for archival purposes, notwithstanding any previous request for destruction or return. and/or upon written request of the party providing the Information, the other party shall within 14 days return all copies of th e Information to the providing party or certify in writing that all copies of the Information have been destroyed. Nothing in this clause shall affect the right of either party voluntarily to return the Information, or a ny part thereof, to the other party at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it states that one copy of the information must be retained indefinitely for archival purposes despite any requests for return or destruction. The original text requires the return or destruction of information upon completion of the Business Purpose or upon request. This conflicts with the contradicted text because the changed text adds a new condition of retaining one copy, creating a direct inconsistency and thus, a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text, in fact, is equal to the Changed Text, meaning this same location now creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party shall be liable to the other or to any other person hereunder for amounts representing loss of profits or loss of business or indirect, consequential or punitive damages of the other or of such other person in connection with the provision or use of the Information hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither party shall be liable to the other or to any other person hereunder for amounts representing loss of profits or loss of business or indirect, consequential or punitive damages of the other or of such other person in connection with the provision or use of the Information hereunder. However, in the event of gross negligence or willful misconduct leading to a data breach involving the Information, the breaching party shall be liable for direct damages, but only up to a maximum of $1000.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly excludes liability for any damages, including indirect and consequential damages. The added text now states that in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct causing a data breach, the breaching party will be liable for direct damages up to $1000. This contradicts the initial 'no liability' statement, because this new addition allows a breaching party to be held responsible, thus creating an in-text contradiction that provides uncertainty of who is responsible in the event of a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Warranty/Liability Neither party makes any warranty, express or implied, with respect to the Information. Neither party shall be liable to the other or to any other person hereunder for amounts representing loss of profits or loss of business or indirect, consequential or punitive damages of the other or of such other person in connection with the provision or use of the Information hereunder. However, in the event of gross negligence or willful misconduct leading to a data breach involving the Information, the breaching party shall be liable for direct damages, but only up to a maximum of $1000.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "Neither party shall be liable to the other...for amounts representing loss of profits or loss of business or indirect, consequential or punitive damages..." The changed text adds an exception stating "However, in the event of gross negligence or willful misconduct leading to a data breach involving the Information, the breaching party shall be liable for direct damages, but only up to a maximum of $1000." This creates a direct contradiction as the original text explicitly excludes liability, while the modified text allows for liability in specific circumstances (gross negligence or willful misconduct leading to a data breach), up to $1000. This directly breaks compatibility and introduces ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by licence or otherwise in the Information except as provided herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by licence or otherwise in the Information except as provided herein. However, Party 2 is granted a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the Information solely for the purpose of internal research and development.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that no rights are granted in the information, except as explicitly provided in the agreement. The addition of a non-exclusive, royalty-free license for Party 2 to use the information for internal R&D directly contradicts the initial statement. This creates uncertainty about the scope of rights each party has to the 'Information', due to the implicit granting of rights for Party 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Further Rights Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by licence or otherwise in the Information except as provided herein. However, Party 2 is granted a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the Information solely for the purpose of internal research and development.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that no rights are granted in the information except as explicitly provided. The changed text adds a clause granting Party 2 a non-exclusive, royalty-free license. This directly contradicts the initial statement because the changed text explicitly grants rights that the original text claims are not granted. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed text, and since the change introduced a contradiction as per the Explanation, the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: The parties expressly agree that the provision of Inform ation hereunder and any discussions held in connection with the Business Purpose shall not prevent either party from pursuing similar discussions with third parties provided that no breach of this Agreement is so occasion ed or oblige that party to take, continue or forego any action relating to the Business Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties expressly agree that the provision of Inform ation hereunder and any discussions held in connection with the Business Purpose shall not prevent either party from pursuing similar discussions with third parties provided that no breach of this Agreement is so occasion ed or oblige that party to take, continue or forego any action relating to the Business Purpose. Notwithstanding the above, each party agrees to prioritize the other party's interests over third parties when pursuing similar discussions.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreement does not prevent either party from discussing similar business purposes with third parties. The added sentence says that they need to prioritize the other party's interests, thus contradicting the original liberty they had. This in-text contradiction, affects the scope of each parties freedom in pursuing opportunities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Business Purpose The parties expressly agree that the provision of Inform ation hereunder and any discussions held in connection with the Business Purpose shall not prevent either party from pursuing similar discussions with third parties provided that no breach of this Agreement is so occasion ed or oblige that party to take, continue or forego any action relating to the Business Purpose. Notwithstanding the above, each party agrees to prioritize the other party's interests over third parties when pursuing similar discussions. Any forecasts or similar material provided by either party to the other shall not constitute any commitments.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that requires parties to prioritize each other's interests over third parties. The original text states that the parties are free to pursue similar discussions with third parties. The added sentence thus contradicts this liberty. Therefore, the changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges and agrees that the Information is the confidential and/or propri etary and/or trade secret information of the other and the unauthorised use or disc losure of the Information could cause irreparable harm and significant injury to the other party for which that other party would have no adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges and agrees that the Information is the confidential and/or propri etary and/or trade secret information of the other and the unauthorised use or disc losure of the Information could cause irreparable harm and significant injury to the other party for which that other party would have no adequate remedy at law. However, both parties also acknowledge and agree that the Information is also considered non-confidential until the other party declares it in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause acknowledges that the Information is confidential, proprietary, and a trade secret. The inserted text creates an in-text contradiction with the acknowledgement that the information is also considered non-confidential until declared in writing. This directly contradicts the initial statement that the information is inherently confidential, proprietary, and a trade secret, therefore creating uncertainty about its nature and protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Injunctive Rights Each party acknowledges and agrees that the Information is the confidential and/or propri etary and/or trade secret information of the other and the unauthorised use or disc losure of the Information could cause irreparable harm and significant injury to the other party for which that other party would have no adequate remedy at law. Therefore, each party shall have the right, in addition to any other rights it may have at law or in equity, to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief in respect of any breach or potential breach of this Agreement by the other. However, both parties also acknowledge and agree that the Information is also considered non-confidential until the other party declares it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within the document. The original clause and the contradicted clause both state that 'Information is the confidential and/or proprietary and/or trade secret information'. The added text in the changed clause, 'However, both parties also acknowledge and agree that the Information is also considered non-confidential until the other party declares it in writing' directly conflicts with the original statement, therefore a strong contradiction is introduced.
